creator: hrant@dadivanyan.net
text:
Thank you!

There are three servers like this:
FreeBSD 13.4-RELEASE-p1 GENERIC amd64
CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2690 v4 @ 2.60GHz (2596.99-MHz K8-class CPU)
  Origin="GenuineIntel"  Id=0x406f1  Family=0x6  Model=0x4f  Stepping=1
Hypervisor: Origin = "VMwareVMware"
real memory  = 17179869184 (16384 MB)
FreeBSD/SMP: 2 package(s) x 2 core(s)

The test host:
FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE-p3 GENERIC amd64
CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2680 v2 @ 2.80GHz (2793.27-MHz K8-class CPU)
  Origin="GenuineIntel"  Id=0x306e4  Family=0x6  Model=0x3e  Stepping=4
Hypervisor: Origin = "VMwareVMware"
real memory  = 17179869184 (16384 MB)
FreeBSD/SMP: 3 package(s) x 2 core(s)

/etc/make.conf in all cases:
OPTIONS_UNSET+=         X11
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=      ssl=openssl
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=      python=3.11
DEFAULT_VERSIONS+=      perl5=5.38

All four systems were always updated using freebsd-update, all ports were always compiled from the sources.

To prepare for the report, on the test host all packages were removed as well as /var/db/ports/* and /usr/ports/*, then ports-mgmt/pkg, ports-mgmt/portmaster, net/gitup installed, then compiled/reinstalled using portmaster after gitup-ing /usr/ports .
At last dns/bind918 was compiled/installed with all its dependencies using portmaster.

There is another case (not in my management) where bare metal host
FreeBSD 13.4-RELEASE-p1 GENERIC amd64
CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) Bronze 3204 CPU @ 1.90GHz (1900.00-MHz K8-class CPU)
  Origin="GenuineIntel"  Id=0x50657  Family=0x6  Model=0x55  Stepping=7
real memory  = 8589934592 (8192 MB)
ACPI APIC Table: <LENOVO THINKSYS>
FreeBSD/SMP: 1 package(s) x 6 core(s)
behaves similarly. bind there was started after mounting /dev in chroot.

Any other information you think could be useful?
